architecture_fit.note: Single base_model-style attention head (QK^T + causal mask + softmax, no MLP); one-hot token-identity QK genuinely computes equality, match_mass=1.0.

baseline_comparison.note: Uniform baseline plotted across the full L sweep; the widening gap (1.0 vs 0.18→0.03) is exactly the real-lookup signal the goal asks for.

faithfulness.note: Two load-bearing causal ablations: dropping self-suppression splits mass ~0.5, zeroing QK identity collapses to uniform; fixes pass_3's p1/p2 oracle leak.

operating_range.note: Holds at exactly 1.0 across L=8..64 (the canonical sweep) but ~1 order of magnitude only; no edge-breaking regime probed.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set weights (one-hot identity embedding, fixed temp and uniform diagonal bias) — full demonstration of mechanism understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Three crisp panels (match-vs-uniform bars, ablation curves, real GPU heatmap with p1/p2 marked) each map to one claim and are legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties each panel to a specific claim — the metric on the y-axis, the shrinking baseline, the faithfulness ablations, the honest heatmap.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built equality-lookup head that perfectly solves the goal (equality_robustness=1.0, row-stochastic to 1e-8) with a genuine token-matching mechanism and no label leak. Faithfulness is well demonstrated through two load-bearing causal ablations, and the three-panel visualisation makes each claim directly checkable. Only knock is operating range: it holds at 1.0 across the canonical L=8..64 sweep but no break point or larger-scale regime is shown.
